No new oversized hub or structural complexity signals remain unaddressed in the candidate list, and there are no new queueable jobs with strong evidence for immediate action.

- State can change quickly if new capsules land or existing hubs accumulate links before the next pass.
- Completed decomposition plans may contain deferred follow-up actions that are easy to miss without explicit review.
- Low visible risk this cycle may hide future complexity if progress updates lag behind graph growth.

## Suggested Actions
- Monitor for new capsule additions or changes in link density.
- Re-evaluate hubs after further progress or summary expansion.
- Review completed decomposition plans for actionable follow-up jobs.
- Continue periodic graph health checks for emerging maintenance targets.
- Hold until new evidence or capsule changes warrant further action.
- Watch for newly added capsules or sharp link-density changes that create fresh oversized hub candidates.
- Re-check hubs after meaningful progress increases or summary expansion that could expose a new decomposition boundary.
- Mine recently completed decomposition reviews for concrete follow-up work only if new evidence appears.
- Continue periodic graph health checks for emerging structural drift or normalization issues.
